summary: The diff implements both REQ-1 and REQ-2 largely as specified. The backend gains a loginWithPassword function that accepts username+password and assigns an admin role without requiring Internet Identity. The frontend gains session management, an updated AdminLogin page, and admin mutations that call ensureAdminAuth before each operation. One concern: credentials are hardcoded in plaintext in frontend/src/lib/adminAuth.ts, and session expiry redirect is only enforced on page load. Overall coverage is strong.

missing_requirements.requirement: If the session is expired or missing, the user is redirected to the login page.
missing_requirements.reason: Session expiry is only checked on AdminPanel mount via a useEffect guard. If the session expires mid-session while the page is still open, admin mutations still fire ensureAdminAuth using hardcoded credentials from adminAuth.ts rather than redirecting the user to the login page. There is no runtime check during mutation failures that redirects to login on session expiry.

hallucinated_features.description: Admin credentials (username and password) are hardcoded in plaintext in frontend/src/lib/adminAuth.ts as constants (ADMIN_USERNAME = 'DeepakKumawat', ADMIN_PASSWORD = 'Deepak@123'), and ensureAdminAuth uses these hardcoded values instead of the user-entered credentials from the login form.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not specify hardcoding credentials client-side. This introduces a pattern where every admin mutation re-authenticates with hardcoded credentials regardless of what the user typed, which was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: A backend/migration.mo module was added to handle actor state migration, introducing adminUsernames (a Map of Principal to Text) and renaming ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH to adminPasswordHash.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention any migration module or schema migration requirement. This is additional infrastructure not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: An initializeWithPassword function was added to the backend to bootstrap the first admin after deployment.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not request a bootstrapping or initialization function. The requirement only asks to fix loginWithPassword to accept username and password without Internet Identity.
